title: Multiply whole numbers and decimals less than 1
description: Multiply decimals and whole numbers like 8x0.2 or 0.56x4
topic_path: Module 2: Multi-digit whole number and decimal fraction operations
Multiply whole numbers and decimals less than 1

hints: There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see three ways we could multiply.

###Place value strategy

We can think in terms of tenths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}18\times 0.4\\\\
&=18 \times 4 \text{ tenths}\\\\
&= 72 \text{ tenths}\\\\
&=7.2
\end{align}$

###Estimation strategy

We can multiply the digits, then use estimation to place the decimal point.

$18\times 4=72$

The decimal $0.4$ is a little less than half. Half of $18$ is $9$, so $18\times 0.4$ is a little less than $9$.

$18\times 0.4=7.2$

###Fraction multiplication strategy

Decimals are a kind of fraction, so we can use fraction multiplication.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{18}\times\greenD{0.4}\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac{18}{1}} \times \greenD{\dfrac{4}{10}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueD{18} \times \greenD{4}}{\blueD{1} \times \greenD{10}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{72}{10}\\\\
&=7.2
\end{align}$

###The answer

$7.2=18 \times 0.4$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see three ways we could multiply.

###Place value strategy

We can think in terms of tenths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}8\times 0.2\\\\
&=8\times 2 \text{ tenths}\\\\
&= 16 \text{ tenths}\\\\
&=1.6
\end{align}$

###Estimation strategy

We can multiply the digits, then use estimation to place the decimal point.

$8\times 2=16$

The decimal $0.2$ is a little less than a fourth. A fourth of $8$ is $2$, so $8\times 0.2$ is a little less than $2$.

$8\times 0.2=1.6$

###Fraction multiplication strategy

Decimals are a kind of fraction, so we can use fraction multiplication.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{8}\times\greenD{0.2}\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac{8}{1}} \times \greenD{\dfrac{2}{10}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueD{8} \times \greenD{2}}{\blueD{1} \times \greenD{10}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{16}{10}\\\\
&=1.6
\end{align}$

###The answer

$1.6=8 \times 0.2$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see three ways we could multiply.

###Place value strategy

We can think in terms of tenths:

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}0.4\times 9\\\\
&=4\text{ tenths}\times9\\\\
&= 36  \text{ tenths}\\\\
&=3.6
\end{align}$

###Estimation strategy

We can multiply the digits, then use estimation to place the decimal point.

$4\times9=36$

The decimal $0.4$ is a little less than half. Half of $9$ is $4.5$, so $0.4\times 9$ is a little less than $4.5$.

$0.4\times 9=3.6$

###Fraction multiplication strategy

Decimals are a kind of fraction, so we can use fraction multiplication.

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueD{0.4}\times\greenD{9}\\\\
&=\blueD{\dfrac{4}{10}} \times \greenD{\dfrac{9}{1}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{\blueD{4} \times \greenD{9}}{\blueD{10} \times \greenD{1}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{36}{10}\\\\
&=3.6
\end{align}$

###The answer

$3.6=0.4 \times 9$
There are many ways to solve this problem. Let's see three ways we could multiply.

###Place value strategy

\end{align}$

We can multiply the digits, then use estimation to place the decimal point.

$3\times25=75$

The value $0.25$ is equal to $\dfrac{1}{4}$.

$\dfrac{1}{4}$ of $3$ is between $0$ and $1$, so $3\times 0.25$ is between $0$ and $1$.

$3\times 0.25=0.75$

###Fraction multiplication strategy

&\phantom{=}\blueD{3}\times\greenD{0.25}\\\\

&= \dfrac{\blueD{3} \times \greenD{25}}{\blueD{1} \times \greenD{100}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{75}{100}\\\\
&=0.75
\end{align}$

###The answer

$3 \times 0.25 = 0.75$
